rationale: The US-Iran conflict is now in its second consecutive day of mutual strikes, with Iran attacking US bases — this is a continued escalation of an already-elevated situation that keeps energy price risk high for Poland, though no qualitative leap beyond the Hormuz closure already in memory.

top_events.title: US-Iran mutual strikes continue, day 2
top_events.summary: The US and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day; Iran claims it attacked US bases following American strikes. This is a continuation of the already-escalated conflict (Hormuz closure), but the sustained mutual military exchange keeps the risk of broader disruption to oil supply — and thus Polish energy prices — elevated.

top_events.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan
top_events.summary: Pakistan conducted air strikes inside Afghanistan, killing civilians and reigniting tensions between the two countries. This is a new regional flare-up in South Asia with no direct impact on Poland, but worth monitoring as a secondary instability factor.

top_events.title: Ukraine strikes military plant deep inside Russia
top_events.summary: Ukraine reports successful missile strikes on a military plant deep inside Russian territory. This is a continuation of Ukraine's deep-strike campaign with no qualitative change — the Russia-Ukraine conflict remains ongoing background noise for Poland.
